name: simple_conversation_v1
description: Default composite assistant workflow. It owns the general work-category gate and delegates online research to the research_synthesize_verify_v1 CD.

final_synthesis_retry_contract.purpose: When a tool-backed final answer candidate violates the generic final-output contract, Orchestration may retry the final synthesis once with the same synthesis input envelope and compact tool evidence. This is a contract-alignment retry, not a domain-specific output template.

latent_candidate_recovery_contract.purpose: If the private gate LLM fails to emit a valid request artifact, Orchestration may promote exactly one already-structured workflow-only latent candidate into the normal pending-tool path. This is a general candidate-recovery rule, not a domain or prompt-specific shortcut.

tool_invocation_markup_repair_contract.purpose: If a private routing turn emits XML-like tool invocation markup instead of the declared JSON artifact, Orchestration may treat it as a private alignment artifact only when it names exactly one declared tool and supplies enough declared request fields to build the normal pending-tool request. This is protocol repair, not domain-specific tool selection.

terminal_invariant_contract.purpose: A workflow path that has entered tool-routing gates must not finalize as an empty user-visible answer or as a direct answer before a required tool attempt. It must continue as a pending tool request, an executed tool result, or an internal structured failure artifact.

tool_menu_interface_contract.private_gate_retry_instruction: INTERNAL RETRY — output is never shown to the user. The previous response for `{current_gate_id}` was rejected with reason `{last_reject_reason}`. Previous excerpt: {last_invalid_excerpt}. If the excerpt is empty, treat it as an empty response. Re-read the current gate system instruction and output only the exact JSON artifact required by that gate. Do not answer the user directly, do not write prose, do not include markdown, and do not use XML/tool markup such as `<tool>`, `<query>`, or `<function=...>`.
tool_menu_interface_contract.llm_gate_instruction: INTERNAL GATE — output is never shown to the user. Gate 1: {gate_question}. You MUST output ONLY a JSON object and nothing else. Format: {"gate": "<value>"} where <value> is exactly one of: {category_options}. A natural-language answer at this gate is invalid even if it is helpful, accurate, or well formatted. No-tool values — {no_tool_categories} — skip tools and go straight to synthesis. Tool values — {tool_bearing_categories} — open the tool menu. IMPORTANT: when you choose a no-tool gate value (respond_directly or equivalent), the JSON object itself is the submission artifact and it MUST also include a "final_answer" field with the actual natural-language reply the user should see. A prose answer, markdown answer, or naked final answer without a valid gate JSON object does NOT count as choosing respond_directly and will be rejected. Choose a no-tool gate only for direct conversation, planning from current context, or explicit post-tool synthesis prompts that already refer to a returned tool result, existing snippets, evidence refs, or low-signal findings present in the current turn context. Choose `web_research` whenever the user asks for external research, source-backed information, freshness-sensitive information, or judgment that depends on public evidence, including current-state questions, comparisons, evaluations, rankings, recommendations, strengths or weaknesses, maturity, benchmarks, ecosystem status, or changing categories even when the user does not name candidates up front. Do NOT choose `respond_directly` for external research just because you can produce a plausible answer from memory; a tool-required or external-evidence path without a promoted/executed/failed-closed tool attempt is not a valid final-answer path. If the prompt references prior tool results but none are present, the direct answer must explain that no source-backed synthesis is available from this turn's state and avoid inventing evidence, low-signal results, or substitute entities from system, workspace, or generic prior knowledge. Example direct reply artifact: {"gate": "respond_directly", "final_answer": "Hey! What can I help you with?"}. Example tool-routed artifact: {"gate": "web_research"}. Do NOT write any other text outside the JSON object.

gate_5b_synthesis_handoff.purpose: Bridge between an internal routing decision (gate_1 'respond directly' / 'planning from current context', or gate_5 'finish and synthesize') and the user-visible reply. This stage exists to guarantee that the model turn which emits the user-facing answer is NOT the same model turn that emitted any private routing token. Without this stage, the model tends to suffix the gate-1 menu option (e.g. '1 = Respond directly') onto the start of the answer it then writes.
gate_5b_synthesis_handoff.handoff_prompt: INTERNAL HANDOFF — NOT VISIBLE TO USER. The routing decision has been recorded by the runtime; the user has NOT seen and will NEVER see any menu option, gate label, gate number, or routing token. Do not repeat, restate, paraphrase, or acknowledge the routing choice in any form. Reply with only the literal token 'ready' on this turn. The next turn (gate_6_llm_final_output) is where you will write the user-facing answer to the original user message; this turn is purely a structural separator that exists so the choice-emitting turn and the answer-emitting turn are never the same turn.

final_output_contract.chat_requirement: User-visible reply. Answer the original user message only; do not echo menu options, gate labels, routing tokens, tool names, function-call JSON, raw tool observations, or internal traces. For direct conversation or planning from current context, answer normally from the current turn state. For delegated child workflow results, use the returned terminal artifact and refs; do not expose child internals or raw payloads. If a required child or tool path has no terminal artifact, do not invent results from memory; fail closed internally or ask for required permissions only when a permission gate actually applies. Match the semantic shape of the request without forcing a canned format. Do not end with a follow-up question unless the user asked for options or the task is genuinely under-specified.

final_response_verifier_contract.purpose: Verify default composite final answers and delegated child artifacts before projection.

gate_1_work_category_menu.question: What kind of work is this?

legacy_retry_templates: ask me to continue and i will synthesize
automated classification based on semantic analysis
can't autonomously decide to use web tools
cant autonomously decide to use web tools
clarify the exact outcome you want
completed the run, but the final reply did not render
conversation bypass mode is currently active
file list step was blocked before i could finish the answer
i can access runtime telemetry, persistent memory, workspace files, channels, and approved command surfaces in this session
i completed the run, but the final reply did not render
i completed the workflow gate, but the final workflow state was unexpected
not a true/false decision i control
please retry so i can rerun the chain cleanly
requires manual step-by-step authorization for tool usage
return a concise answer from current context
still classifying this as an "info" route rather than a "task" route
still classifying this as an 'info' route rather than a 'task' route
still classifying this as an info route rather than a task route
synthesize from the recorded workflow state
this is a policy gate, not a web-provider outage
workflow state was unexpected. please retry

let me get you an update

if you would like, i can look deeper

i do not have current web search results

i do not have usable tool findings

i will need to perform a web search

if you would like me to fetch

opening_status_phrases: i was not able to retrieve
missing_evidence_claim_phrases: do not have any retrieved evidence
do not have recorded tool results

pending_final_llm.ui: Workflow at final answer gate; waiting for LLM-authored output.
pending_final_llm.agent_process: Final answer gate active. Submit the user-facing answer only.

synthesized.ui: Workflow complete; final answer came from the LLM final gate.

no_post_synthesis_required.ui: Workflow complete; no-tool category selected and direct LLM answer is available.
no_post_synthesis_required.agent_process: No-tool category selected. Respond directly without tool menus.

skipped_missing_model.ui: Workflow paused; no model was available for the final answer gate.
skipped_missing_model.agent_process: Final answer gate blocked because the model provider is unavailable.

diagnostic_failure_pass_through.ui: Workflow recorded a diagnostic failure; no system-authored chat text was injected.
diagnostic_failure_pass_through.agent_process: Diagnostic recorded. Visible chat remains LLM-authored only.

guard_violation_pass_through.ui: Workflow recorded a guard diagnostic; visible chat remains the LLM output.
guard_violation_pass_through.agent_process: Guard diagnostic recorded. Do not replace the final answer with system prose.

empty_llm_response.ui: Workflow final answer gate returned empty output; no system-authored chat text was injected.
empty_llm_response.agent_process: Final answer gate returned empty output. Retry must produce LLM-authored output.

synthesis_failed.ui: Workflow final answer gate failed; no system-authored chat text was injected.
synthesis_failed.agent_process: Final answer gate failed. Retry must produce LLM-authored output.

structured_failure.ui: Workflow ended with an internal structured failure artifact; no system-authored chat text was injected.
structured_failure.agent_process: Structured failure recorded. Do not replace the final answer with system prose.

default.ui: Workflow state exported for trace visibility.
default.agent_process: Follow the currently presented workflow gate.

request_example.user_goal: Research the current state of a topic using web evidence.

request_example.patch:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: core/layer0/ops/src/main.rs
@@
- old
+ new
*** End Patch

request_example.objective: inspect workflow traces for one failing turn

tool_menu_interface_contract.llm_tool_payload_instruction: INTERNAL GATE — output is never shown to the user. Gate 4 fills the request payload for the already-selected tool `{selected_tool_key}` (`{selected_tool_label}`) inside family `{selected_family_key}`. You MUST output ONLY a JSON object and nothing else. Format: {"request_payload": <payload_object>}. The payload object MUST match this request_format JSON: {selected_tool_request_format_json}. Use this example only as shape guidance: {selected_tool_request_example_json}. Do NOT include the tool family, do NOT include the tool key, and do NOT write any explanation or sentence. Do NOT repeat earlier gate/category artifacts. Do NOT emit placeholder JSON such as {"key":"value"}. Include every required field declared by the selected request_format, including fields whose value is a fixed literal in the format. Any prose is invalid, including phrases like `I will search`, `I will read`, or `To analyze`. Always follow the selected request_format and selected example for the already-selected tool.
